name: SW3 Splice
description: Fully analog 2-channel click-free signal splicer/switcher. Each channel picks between A and B via three switching modes (equal-level, equal-slope, hard) with manual or CV/gate control, at switching rates up to 200 kHz.

controls.name: A / Auto / B (Input select)
controls.description: Forces input A, input B, or Auto where the module toggles when A and B momentarily match.
controls.detail: 3-position toggle. In Auto, the switch flips at every moment A and B cross, so a mono signal self-splices instead of being manually chosen. Up = A, down = B, center = Auto.

controls.name: 1 / /2 / /4 (Toggle division)
controls.description: Divides how often the auto-toggle actually flips — every crossing, every other, or every fourth.
controls.detail: Acts as a sub-harmonic generator when the signal is an oscillator: /2 and /4 introduce octaves/sub-octaves into the composite output. Works in Auto mode and with the D (gate) input.

controls.name: X / = / H (Splice mode)
controls.description: Chooses the click-free rule: X = match when equal, = = match when equal and slopes align, H = hard switch.
controls.detail: X waits for level equality before flipping (click-free on most signals). = is stricter and waits for equal level AND same slope direction (cleanest on audio). H ignores matching and hard-switches immediately — use for percussive effects or when driven by gates.

controls.name: Stereo mode switches
controls.description: Link channel 2 to channel 1 (stereo), swap the two outputs, or keep them as two independent mono switches.
controls.detail: Lets you use the module as: dual mono (two separate switches), stereo-linked (one decision drives both OUTs so a stereo pair switches together), or swap (channel 2 inverts the A/B choice, useful for stereo panning/ping-pong).

inputs.name: A1
inputs.voltage: audio or CV, ~100 kΩ input impedance

inputs.name: B1
inputs.voltage: audio or CV, ~100 kΩ input impedance

inputs.name: A2
inputs.voltage: audio or CV, ~100 kΩ input impedance

inputs.name: B2
inputs.voltage: audio or CV, ~100 kΩ input impedance

inputs.name: D (Drive)
inputs.description: External control input for switching logic. With mode switch on H, this gate/CV chooses A or B directly; in X/= modes it can force reconsideration.
inputs.voltage: gate / CV, up to 200 kHz switching

inputs.name: R (Reset)
inputs.description: Reset input — resets the auto-toggle flip-flop and division counter to a known state.

outputs.name: OUT1
outputs.description: Channel 1 spliced/switched output. Carries whichever of A1/B1 is currently selected.
outputs.voltage: audio or CV, 3.3 kΩ / 20 Ω through-switch impedance

outputs.name: OUT2
outputs.description: Channel 2 spliced/switched output. Independent, linked-to-1, or swapped depending on stereo switches.
outputs.voltage: audio or CV, 3.3 kΩ / 20 Ω through-switch impedance

outputs.name: ON1
outputs.description: Logic output for channel 1. Goes to ~5V whenever input A1 is the one currently selected.

outputs.name: ON2
outputs.description: Logic output for channel 2. Goes to ~5V whenever input A2 is the one currently selected.

behaviors.name: Click-free equal-level splice (X mode)
behaviors.trigger: mode switch in X, Auto selected
behaviors.description: Flip from A to B only happens at instants where A and B are at the same voltage. Prevents the discontinuity that causes clicks when cross-fading full-range signals.

behaviors.name: Slope-matched splice (= mode)
behaviors.description: Flip only when A and B are equal AND moving in the same direction. Cleanest possible splice on audio — you rarely hear the transition at all.

behaviors.name: Hard switch (H mode)
behaviors.trigger: mode switch in H, D input driven
behaviors.description: Immediate A/B swap on the rising edge of D. No click avoidance. Use for rhythmic wave-chopping and percussive bursts.

behaviors.name: Division / sub-harmonic
behaviors.trigger: division switch on /2 or /4, audio through A and B
behaviors.description: In Auto mode, only every 2nd or 4th crossing triggers a flip, producing a sub-octave alternation on periodic signals — a sub-harmonic generator.

behaviors.name: Stereo link
behaviors.description: Both channels follow channel 1's control decision simultaneously — a stereo signal (L → A1/A2, R → B1/B2) switches as a pair.

behaviors.name: Channel-swap stereo
behaviors.description: Channel 2 mirrors channel 1 with A/B swapped. Patch left to A1/B2 and right to B1/A2 for hard stereo ping-pong on the same gate.

behaviors.name: ON logic output
behaviors.description: ON1/ON2 emit a +5V gate while A is active — use them as trigger sources, clock dividers, or to drive envelopes in sync with the splice.

patchIdeas.name: Click-free crossfade between two oscillators
patchIdeas.patch: Replace a crossfader: hard-pick between two VCOs with zero pop, using equal-slope mode so the transition is inaudible.
steps: Patch VCO 1 → SW3 A1.
Patch VCO 2 → SW3 B1.
Set channel 1 mode switch to '=' (equal level + equal slope).
Set channel 1 division to '1'.
Set channel 1 input select to 'A' (start on VCO 1).
Patch SW3 OUT1 → VCA IN → mixer.
Flip the A/B switch manually, or patch a slow gate to D input and set input select to 'Auto' to let the module wait for a clean crossing.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT1 — the selected VCO at full level, mono, no click.
patchIdeas.hear: A completely seamless swap between the two oscillators — you'll hear the new pitch but no pop or glitch at the transition, even mid-note.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SW3 Splice:
* CH1 mode: =
* CH1 div: 1
* CH1 sel: Auto

- VCO 1 (Out) -> SW3 (A1)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> SW3 (B1)
- Gate Source (Out) >> SW3 (D)
- SW3 (OUT1) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Sub-octave from a single VCO
patchIdeas.patch: Use Auto mode with /2 or /4 division to splice a VCO with itself inverted, producing clean sub-harmonic octaves.
steps: Patch VCO saw out → SW3 A1.
Patch the same VCO through an inverter (or the inverted out of a mult/attenuverter) → SW3 B1.
Set channel 1 input select to 'Auto' (center).
Set channel 1 mode to 'X' (equal-level).
Set channel 1 division to '/2' for one octave down, or '/4' for two octaves down.
Patch SW3 OUT1 → mixer channel.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT1 — sub-octave of the input VCO, same waveform character.
patchIdeas.hear: A clean sub-octave that tracks the VCO 1:1. /2 gives one octave down, /4 gives two. Switching back to /1 returns the original pitch — great for bass thickening under a lead.

patchIdeas.name: Stereo ping-pong with one gate
patchIdeas.patch: Use the swap stereo mode to bounce a mono source between L and R at gate speed, synced to your clock.
steps: Patch mono source → SW3 A1 and → SW3 B2 (so A1=source, B2=source).
Patch silence (or a second source) → SW3 B1 and → SW3 A2.
Set the stereo switch to 'swap'.
Set both channels' mode to 'H' (hard switch) — we want sharp rhythmic moves.
Patch a clock or rhythmic gate → D input.
Patch SW3 OUT1 → stereo mixer L, SW3 OUT2 → stereo mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT1 / OUT2 — hard-panned ping-pong of the source, following the gate.
patchIdeas.hear: Each gate pulse jumps the source from left to right speaker and back. Tempo-sync the gate for rhythmic panning; use a fast LFO for a tremolo-pan.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SW3 Splice:
* Stereo: swap
* CH1 & CH2 mode: H

- Source (Out) -> SW3 (A1)
- Source (Out) -> SW3 (B2)
- Clock (Out) >> SW3 (D)
- SW3 (OUT1) -> Mixer (L)
- SW3 (OUT2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Gate from an audio-rate splice (ON output trick)
patchIdeas.patch: Turn the ON logic output into a rhythmic gate source derived from the music itself — no sequencer needed.
steps: Patch a melodic source → SW3 A1.
Patch silence (unpatched) or a second drone → SW3 B1.
Set channel 1 mode to 'X', division to '/4', input select to 'Auto'.
Patch SW3 ON1 → envelope generator Trigger in.
Patch envelope → VCA CV controlling anything (a hi-hat, a reverb send).
patchIdeas.signalOut: ON1 — +5V gate pulses every 4th zero-crossing of the source.
patchIdeas.hear: Rhythmic gates that lock to the melodic content feeding A1 — faster notes produce faster gates. A free 'groove follower' for triggering percussion or accents off a bassline.
